free_text: Study of activation cross-sections of deuteron
induced reactions on rhodium up to 40 MeV
High purity 0.0123 mm Rh  foils
were used interleaved with Al 0.1  mm
monitor foils.
The irradiation of
the targets was performed in He gas atmosphere in a
water-cooled, Faraday-cup like target holder. For
determination of the beam intensity and the energy.
The target stack was irradiated with 40 MeV incident
energy, with collimated proton beam for 30 min, at
about 24 nA. The radioactivity of each sample and
monitor foil was measured nondestructively by HPGe
c-spectrometry twice. A first counting was started
about 20 h after the end of the bombardment (EOB),
the second series about 7-10 days after EOB. The
decay data were taken from NUDAT.
Chapter 4
The uncertainty of the energy of the
bombarding beam in the middle of each irradiated foil
was estimated combining the uncertainties of the
energy degradation depending on the thickness of the
target foils in the stack and the energy straggling.
The uncertainties of the cross-section
values were estimated in the standard way: the
independent relative errors of the linearly
contributing processes were summed quadratically and
the square root of the sum was taken. The average
uncertainty was found  to be 12-14%.
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS, EMPIRE
and ALICE-IPPE.
The measured experimental data were compared
to those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
calculation of thick target yields.
